hint_1: Think about this as two main steps: finding word boundaries in the text, then mapping each word to its vocabulary ID while tracking positions.
hint_2: Use regex pattern '\b\w+\b' to find word boundaries. The re.finditer() method returns match objects with start() and end() methods for positions.
hint_3: Structure: 1) Check for empty text, 2) Find all matches with _find_token_matches(), 3) For each match: normalize with _normalize_token(), lookup in vocab with .get(normalized, vocab['<UNK>']), append dict with token/id/start/end
